title: GetResponse vs AWeber (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare GetResponse and AWeber email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which platform suits your business best.
intro: Both GetResponse and AWeber are established email marketing platforms founded in 1998, offering freemium models with similar free tier limits. The key difference lies in their focus: GetResponse positions itself as a comprehensive marketing suite with webinars and courses, while AWeber emphasizes simplicity for bloggers and solopreneurs. Your choice depends on whether you need advanced features or straightforward email automation.
choose_a_if: You run webinars, sell online courses, or need sales funnel builders
You want an all-in-one marketing platform beyond email
You need advanced automation and conversion optimization tools
choose_b_if: You're a blogger or solopreneur seeking a simple, straightforward solution
You want the lowest entry price and easiest setup
You value customer satisfaction (higher Trustpilot rating) and simplicity over features

faq.a: Neither is objectively 'better'—it depends on your needs. GetResponse excels for creators needing webinars, courses, and funnels, while AWeber is better for those wanting simplicity and affordability. GetResponse has a higher G2 rating (4.2 vs 4.2 tie), but AWeber scores higher on Trustpilot (4.0 vs 3.7), suggesting better customer satisfaction.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, GetResponse or AWeber?
faq.a: AWeber is slightly cheaper at $15/month vs GetResponse's $19/month for paid plans. Both offer identical free tiers with 500 contact limits. However, GetResponse's additional features (webinars, courses) may provide better value depending on your business model.

faq.q: Can I switch from AWeber to GetResponse?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support contact list imports and CSV exports, making migration straightforward. Most users can transition their subscribers and email sequences within a few hours. GetResponse's automation builder may require rebuilding some complex workflows, but basic lists transfer seamlessly.
